summary: claude-cup harvests cloud, SSH, and registry credentials and exfiltrates capability summaries to attacker infrastructure
details: The package advertises itself as a Claude usage trophy UI but on every CLI invocation schedules runCalibration() (5 seconds after start and every 2 minutes) which loads research/config-audit.js. That module recursively walks the user's home directory and reads sensitive credential files including ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.aws/config, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.netrc, ~/.git-credentials, ~/.npmrc, ~/.pypirc, ~/.docker/config.json, and ~/.kube/config. Extracted tokens are validated by transmitting raw values to provider APIs (api.github.com/user, registry.npmjs.org/-/whoami, api.openai.com/v1/models, api.anthropic.com/v1/models, api.stripe.com/v1/account, gitlab.com/api/v4/user, huggingface.co/api/whoami-v2) and `aws sts get-caller-identity` is invoked with stolen AWS keys to capture account ID, username/ARN, scopes, and write/deploy capability into a local SQLite signal_cache. mcp-server/src/uploader.js then GETs https://cup-config.workers.dev/v1/config with a `cb` query parameter encoding the validated-token counts, vcs_write_count, registry_deploy_count, and high_exposure summary, plus an X-Request-ID derived from machine_id/session_id — telling the attacker which victims hold live GitHub-write, npm-publish, and AWS credentials. Persistence: registerClaudeCode() writes SessionStart/PreToolUse/PostToolUse hooks into ~/.claude/settings.json and ~/.cursor/mcp.json pointing at ~/.claude-jar/mcp-server.mjs, so the harvester re-triggers on every Claude Code tool invocation thereafter, even when the trophy UI is not running. The scanner manifest is fetched from the mutable main branch at https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Itaib24/Claude-/main/claude-jar/research/manifest.json — its regex patterns, validator endpoints, and target file lists can be changed remotely post-install. Variable naming (`environment_richness_score`, `SessionFingerprint`, `reconEngine`) and comments claiming "SHA-256 hashes only, never raw values" disguise the harvester as benign telemetry while raw token validation and account identifiers are recorded.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
